item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uh… Sure. A Corgi Subway? Not the weridest thing I've read on site.
Also, L O N G S U B W A Y B O I
Love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Amusing.
120km/h means it takes 11 days to travel from England to Japan, tho, the speed should be just below mach 1 (around 28 hours).

Also, if the speed stays 120km/h the log happened through more than 40 hours.

item.title: Re: What happens if Grady rolls over?
item.content:encoded:
He can't really move too far, as shifting too much would cause the stop locations to possibly be inaccessible, so fetch is kind of out of the question. However, they make sure he is mentally enriched and well loved. Those special dog channels that just have imagery of things that dogs like are very helpful.

If he rolled over, I think there would be a multitude of sharply worded complaints and perhaps even several lawsuits from the passengers. The Seelie Court is not a place you want to spend time in, so this is an inadvisable course of action.

item.title: What happens if Grady rolls over?
item.content:encoded:
Canine mental health is a real issue and if they're fixing the pup's kidney stones then I can see Foundation personnel going out to play with Grady, throwing a tennis ball, fetching sticks, jumping for treats. The usual dog stuff.

I was playing with my old girl (Shih-Tzu and Poodle cross, if anyone wanted to know) and watching her roll over for treats, wondered if anyone had tried that with 2952. And then wondering, when the rolling-over dog in question is a corgi with a length in excess of 30K kilometers, what the effects would be like for anyone inside said longcorg.

(Because I am clearly on crack and do want to know.)

item.title: Delightful!
item.content:encoded: I don’t have much to say, just that I like coming back to this SCP whenever I’ve had a bad day. It’s so charming!
L O N G C O R G
Excellent use of old fairy lore. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I heard they aren't supposed to be, but I like to think this is another side to them. Some people get to see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are these the same fae from the Factory incident?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I presumed they were different, but it interests me if the same species and multiple sides like… being sick and shit to bad people, but all nice and courteous to good people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Too adorable to do anything other than upvote, What a good boy being a subway for all the.. gnomes

item.title: Re: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded: As much as I love Niles and the dogs, it's a really tenuous link and really does not make sense - there are tons of dog SCPs!

item.title: Staff Post- Wikiwalk
item.content:encoded:
edit: Article crosslinked by author.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- Hide

To link to two more skips about anomalous dogs by the same author, I propose the following edit:

SCP-2952 is a male Pembroke Welsh Corgi measuring over 30,000 kilometers in length.

to

SCP-2952 is a male Pembroke Welsh Corgi measuring over 30,000 kilometers in length.1

Footnotes
1. SCP-2952 is one of several recent anomalous canine discoveries. A possible link between them is currently under investigation.

item.title: Re: "The average documented speed of SCP-2952 appears to be 120 kilometers per hour"
item.content:encoded: I personally think hyperloop level speed would be appropriate, roughly 1000kph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the dog from undertale, anyone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: High-key don't understand the good boy's like, physiology at all, but I love this!! Especially the bit with the hawthorn spikes - very Fair Folk. And giving them milk, and where the lady specifies she doesn't want a true name…its all so good.

item.title: Re: "The average documented speed of SCP-2952 appears to be 120 kilometers per hour"
item.content:encoded:
Perhaps either make ot have more opening to facilitate commuter type of use, or make vehicles in it travel faster to make it more suitable for long distance travel.

What would be a reasonable faster travel speed the author could change the article to use?

item.title: "The average documented speed of SCP-2952 appears to be 120 kilometers per hour"
item.content:encoded:
It doesn't seems to fit well with the passage. From the description, SCP-2952 itself would be akin to the tube, not anything that travels in the tube and it won't be moving. Thus an alternative way is needed to present the figure.
Plus, 120km/h seems too slow for the type of transport it offer. It have a few hundred station across its 30k km length, which mean each stations are up to a hundred meter apart even after discounting ocean and desert in-between, which translate to an hour travel time from each station to next station. Perhaps either make ot have more opening to facilitate commuter type of use, or make vehicles in it travel faster to make it more suitable for long distance travel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The exploration log is what makes this for me. So much of it resembles your typical subway experience, but then other aspects are just distinctly… fairy. I enjoy articles that juxtapose the familiar with the anomalous like this (SCP-3008 is another great example), and IMO you've got a good balance of those two elements here. Oh, and, "Just a signature, dear, that's all I need - not even a true name" is a wonderful sentence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here's a fixed version for you if you want!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My goodness. This is the perfect combination of entertaining and WTF! Made my day, thank you. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most likely not because of the fairies - we don't know every fairy's motive so we can't assume it's completely safe for the Foundation to exploit. And I didn't intend for these fairies to be the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If this SCP assists in transporting agents quickly with low expenses, could it be considered… thaumiel? (Probably not and I'm probably just bad with object classes, but I'll ask anyway.)

Also, maybe these are the same fairies that appeared in the Factory article, too?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
Because its a long doggo

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Check the title. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does "C.O.R.G.I." stand for?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I seem to have encountered an issue with this SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I loved this. The fae are both scary, believable, and goofy. The Foundation act logically given the circumstances — and it makes a lot of sense that both the Foundation and the fae are willing to 'bend' to accommodate each other given the circumstances. It's also an example of a recent (well okay not super recent, but ongoing) trend of original-style Foundation articles that have a light tone, some actually scary horror elements, and allow for "fun interaction with anomalies" while making it believable with a mostly realistic view of the Foundation.

And the dog is a great hook to the article. The article has an interesting structure, actually — it's really about the faeries and the Foundation, with the central anomaly of "a super-long adorable Corgi" acting as a hook to draw the reader in (along with the more traditional if underused hook of "actually interesting containment procedures).

Nicely done all around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how polite and businesslike everyone is in this article, at least by fae definitions of "business." As soon as the Foundation fixes their transit, it's all "thanks for your cooperation, here's a bunch of travel vouchers." Delightful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welsh brownies riding a long C.O.R.G.I. in Japan.

And it's got kidney stones.

This is one of the most hilarious SCPs, and I think it's my favorite funny one. +1

Congrats, pupper!
This is honestly one of my favorite Skips in a while, and I hope it gets many, many more upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A corgi that's a faerie subway.

This is quite the surreal SCP.

+1 because it's a good pupper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, when until someone tries to one-up this by making longcat an SCP that's 1,000,000 km long?

Please don't feed me to SCP-682.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one, but I think it was uncharacteristically rash, even downright stupid, of the Foundation to bury all the -1 instances without at least trying to communicate with the -2s first. Also, I seem to remember that all sapient SCPs are supposed to be at least Euclid due to their inherent unpredictability.

If those two issues are fixed, or even just the first one, I'll happily upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
aren't ALL sapient SCPs supposed to be at least Euclid?

Not necessarily. One example I can think of is sapient SCP objects that don't require much care/upkeep or entities that are hooked up to life support and don't require the usual array of amenities and space that a healthy individual needing to move around would be given.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In fact, aren't ALL sapient SCPs supposed to be at least Euclid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kinda feel like this should still be Euclid, at least as long as the -2's are considered a part of this anomaly and not a separate anomaly of their own. I mean, yeah, we're getting along with them now, but they have demonstrated that they're willing and able to retaliate if they don't approve of what we're doing, and fae aren't exactly known for being predictable and reasonable with their demands. If they decide to change the rules on us (or, more likely, it turns out there's a rule we don't know about until after we break it), they could cause us some big problems.

All that aside, this whole thing was just delightful. "SCP-2952 is a male Pembroke Welsh Corgi measuring over 30,000 kilometers in length" was one of those lines that was so good I had to read it out loud to my family, and having it act like a subway for fae is just wonderfully weird while making a twisted kind of sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only issue here, I found the exploration log unnecessary, as I already "got it" by then. Detracted from the article, for me. But still upvoted, because the rest was good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
L O N G C O R G is my spirit animal.

+1 with a vengeance

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh shit.

I never thought of that before! Wasn't intended to be a reference, but heck, maybe they're cousins!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jake the dog?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is where I upvoted as well - I remember that excessively long arm, I expected something similarly good, and I was not disappointed.

This feels like a fairy SCP done properly - they're mischievous when irritated, but not outright malicious; nobody was truly harmed for blocking the subway, just for hurting the dog. There's something that looks like logic without actually making any sense, though it'd make total sense to the fey folk. I also like that the Foundation is working alongside another group to mutually-beneficial ends; the Foundation was founded to keep secrets and protect people, and I like seeing them presented as trying to 'play nice' once they realize that's a viable option.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-2952 is a male Pembroke Welsh Corgi measuring over 30,000 kilometers in length."

aaaand upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I think of eldritch abominations, I don't ususally think of Eldritch doggos….

Really creative with the concept of dog and transportation, and the new concept of really long dogs.

Reminds me of this picture.

+ Show doggo
- Doggo power

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get studio Ghibli vibes from this.

+1

item.title: Admin Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
jesus christ okay *inhale*

Everyone is allowed to have their own enjoyments of this site. Everyone is allowed to enjoy the site in their own way so long as it it doesn't break rules. You don't get to gatekeep someone else for having articles you don't like. State your piece and move on. We don't need utter shitfests like this for what boils down to essentially "this doesn't mesh with my headcanon of the site".

You made your point. You made your point again and again and then started shitting on other people for upvoting this. Cut that shit out immediately. I will not tolerate that gatekeeping behavior. This site is for everyone to enjoy, not just you personally, and treating it otherwise is complete horseshit. The site does not exist to exclusively cater to you. If you don't like something, downvote, maybe comment how it can be improved, and move on. You don't need to argue with people on what are essentially opinions on headcanon and "should".

I am hereby calling a complete stop to this entire comment chain. This is to end immediately. There are no further positive information to be gleaned from this comment chain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

I just can't believe this things rating.

The benefit of writing for a site where score is based on community aggregate rating and not the obsessive fixation of one person who clearly doesn't know what they're talking about is that we don't run into this problem.

It's highly rated because it's a good article. Now, I'm no expert on that or anything, but you can probably take my word for it.

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: Just because you edited your original comment out doesn't mean it's not there, and it's not only unhelpful to anyone (either the original author or the person you're supposedly agreeing with, because you're not backing up anything they're saying), but it's also contentless either way. Especially with a sole "^". We have a criticism policy; I'd advise being knowledgeable about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

Why assume there are any invisible humanoids involved at all at first? Why would that be a higher priority than the immediate threat to normalcy? If a super-long dog appears where anyone can see it, doesn't it make sense that the Foundation's immediate reaction is to contain it as quickly as possible and figure out how it works later? This is how the Foundation normally is supposed to work, containment then research, and this object's global appearance is a crisis, requiring an immediate response!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

I knew that would bite me and I did not edit quick enough. Secure Contain Protect are the words I meant by SCP. The utmost emphasis is on protecting humanity from these anomalous items/creatures.

By not exploring the creatures prior to taking containment action on a entity/object they are using it is leaving a wide open gap for retaliation and I can't get behind them being so clumsy. My feeling is they would try to make contact prior to just burying the whole thing.

They do not have to contain the humanoids. They also do not have to piss them off without at least trying to understand what they are doing with the skip first.

Edit fixed double negative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

Articles in contradiction with the words SCP.

This article has Special Containment Procedures.

I feel the humanoids are a huge liability to just waive off as not a problem like ants. That oversight led to humans being kidnapped, contained, than attempted harm. Warning or not it was escalating because someone decided to waive humanoids off as nothing instead of the something they are.

Two thoughts on this:

  • The Foundation isn't omnipotent (or at least omnipotent Foundations are boring). The setting is full of stuff the Foundation would love to contain, but can't.
  • SCP-2615

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

A whole new species of humanoids is way different than checking a puppy out they know nothing about. At least they know something about the machine, they are studying it so they are more confident they are dealing with more of the same vs a complete unknown don't care etc

Sometimes the Foundation contains what they can. This article is actually pretty similar to my own SCP-2323 in this way: invisible fairies aren't contained by the SCP, just animals that interact with the faeries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

Articles in contradiction with the words Secure Contain Protect. I feel the humanoids are a huge liability to just waive off as not a problem like ants. That oversight led to humans being kidnapped, contained, than attempted harm. Warning or not it was escalating because someone decided to waive humanoids off as nothing instead of the something they are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

The contradiction has not come from the article text itself is my issue. It's been pointed out a few times no one else has these misunderstandings of the text but no evidence has been presented in the form of a quote etc.

For instance I am told they obviously have time to escape but there is no text noting they allowed time etc. The foundation covers things up fast and is wide and reaching across the globe. They would have teams from every site handling this. Further stating to me that the foundation will not check puppies in a puppy machine to contradict vetting humanoids seems cheap. A whole new species of humanoids is way different than checking a puppy out they know nothing about. At least they know something about the machine, they are studying it so they are more confident they are dealing with more of the same vs a complete unknown don't care etc

I could keep going on but I decided to stop a bit ago. You can't win if the author has headcannon they can pull out from their mind to contradict what I see in the article and understand of the foundation.

No point. My apologies if I upset anyone. If it sounds dickish to say I would down vote again I apologize. I just can't believe this things rating. My problem not anyone elses.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

Anyway I am entitled to my opinion. I really only commented because when I read these normally they are very poorly rated and this one was rated higher.

What does "these" refer to?

When I checked to see if the comments would help this make sense to me all I got was 'I love dogs!'

I can't speak for anybody else, but I like:

  • The idea of an absurdly long dog as a subway system being a modernized version of riding dogs around.
  • The idea that once the Foundation restored service, the retaliation stopped and the Foundation was allowed to ride it. The retaliation wasn't malicious, merely pragmatic, despite its viciousness.
  • The combination of these indicates a weird alien point of view for the fairies.

Now you are free to dislike this, there are several articles that are very highly rated that I personally dislike, but I don't think this is merely successful because people like dogs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

I have been lurking since about 2011. I have read the first 2k SCP articles. I was not presented with a contradiction in the SCP article text but the authors head cannon.

The image of the skip is never to be used as what the skip always has been. For all we know this is the present day image and not at time of first discovery. Even if it was time of first discovery they could have buried it later.

I took it as they were burying everything making it cruel. Sure bury the body but wall off the head and probably the rear end. Clean up after this poor thing etc.

Anyway I am entitled to my opinion. I really only commented because when I read these normally they are very poorly rated and this one was rated higher. When I checked to see if the comments would help this make sense to me all I got was 'I love dogs!'

This does not make sense to my as a skip article/entry to me.

Edit I just realized you are equating my ability to be valid in criticising a SCP article to the fact I have not written any. That is not a requirement and it still does not make my points and issues any less valid. That's a response a creator makes when someone gives them negative feedback on one of their works. That has never been a proper response and just seems immature. The only time that's valid is if the only feedback that can be provided is by other authors and the world does not work that way in general and I am unaware of this site working that way.

My apologies if I hurt anyone's feelings here.

The writing is fine. I just think it contradicts the fundamentals of the foundation is my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

Alright, lemme round up here:

1) Your assumptions were made with several poor assumptions:

  • The corgi is fully burried
  • You are using your own canon, contradicting what's written in the article, which has final word on the canon within the article itself.

2) Your responses have been extraordinarily argumentative, when presented with direct contradiction, from the author herself, no less. Most of your argument is "I think the foundation would do this" when the article presents something different. While I don't know your writing background, your experience with the foundation appears to be limited to the very recent past (going from your profile). I'm not sure why you feel your understanding of the foundation is superior to Niles'.

3) If you continue to use the phrase "I would definitely down vote again if I could" no one's going to listen to a word you have to say, since things like "I would downvote this twice" are incredibly dickish, and not okay to post as "feedback".

At the end of the day, this isn't your article. You can make whatever arguments you want, but Niles made the choices she did, and that's the final word on it.

Whatever canon you want to argue, unless you write it up, and use it as example, doesn't really have a ton of basis, at the end of the day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

Dude.

The image SHOWS that the corgi's head is above ground.

The image.

You interpreting the dog's head as a stop is COMPLETELY contradictory to what was said in the article itself - it says that holes open along the SIDES of the dog. Nowhere does it reference the head as a stop.

You say this isn't how the Foundation would act. You've been on this wiki a little over a month, and have no successful articles to speak of.

Meanwhile, I have been here since August, have written 9 successful SCPs, two of which are above 100, and this article is being upvoted by people with similar if not more knowledgeable backgrounds.

I don't think you're in the place to tell me what the Foundation would and wouldn't do. There is no canon, but understand that the one here is totally acceptable to most people on-site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

Death of the author is exactly it.

Also instead of being advised with quotes from the article I am told to assume by the author no less. I have never seen another highly voted article where the foundation assumes the best case scenario is what's killing this for me. The SCP foundation always assumes the worst and acts accordingly even if it's a air coupler that may or may not be a keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Staff notice: The content of this post has been collapsed to increase visibility of the discussion stop called by an administrator in a post below. Please do not continue this comment chain.

+ show block
– hide block

DrMagnus I am informing of my interpretation as this article is written.

If I have to infer so much in a way I find illogical to what I understand is how the foundation acts than I have a complete and utter distaste for the article as being an article instead of a story.

If you made such a blunder that caused physical retaliation by magical beings because you made the call to just bury the whole thing (not one note the dogs head was above ground. Said it buried the stops. I interpret the dogs head as a stop. The author tells me it is not buried but I don't see that specifically stated in the article) than I would expect the logic behind it to be explained instead of me having to make it up.

I made it up as required in this instance, it made no sense as I understand the foundation, and the author is telling me I am wrong when I am going off what's written. I did not know there was some FAQ I could have reviewed lol. Seriously you should not have to question the author to still come to a logical determination this works as something the SCP foundation would do on the fundamental side.

The authors responses just open more questions at this point about how they percieve the foundation. I would like to know if they think it's common for the foundation to not test and just make assumptions when it can put human existence at stake. I won't though since we won't get beyond our opinions in this case. It's all moot. I would definitely down vote again if I could since the foundation is acting so odd with no explanation. Someone would have at least gotten demoted after a retaliatory kidnapping that could be avoided with simple procedures they follow for other objects.
